steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The first sentence signals that Feldmann and Baum both received the same award "though for different reasons." That phrase telegraphs a contrast: the second sentence must highlight the difference between their reasons. The blank transition must mark a shift or contrast between Feldmann (honored as an art historian) and Baum (recognized as a photographer).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — for instance,: "For instance" introduces an example of a previously stated idea. It does not mark a contrast, so it cannot signal the difference between the two reasons. ✗
  • Choice B — by contrast,: "By contrast" directly signals that what follows differs from what preceded — Baum's recognition as a photographer contrasts with Feldmann's recognition as an art historian. This matches the "though for different reasons" signal in the first sentence. ✓
  • Choice C — in other words,: "In other words" restates or clarifies something already said. The second sentence is not a restatement of Feldmann's honor; it introduces new information about Baum. ✗
  • Choice D — as a result,: "As a result" marks a causal relationship, implying Baum's recognition was caused by Feldmann's. There is no causal link stated or implied. ✗
B is correct.
